oneLiner: Healthy, refreshing protein drinks that fit beautifully into your everyday lifestyle.
positioningStatement: PEP is a premium protein drink designed as a lifestyle accessory for wellness-focused individuals seeking a healthy, refreshing treat in their daily routines. With light, vibrant flavours and a visual identity rooted in minimal, pastel aesthetics, PEP supports balance and self-image without the heaviness or seriousness of gym drinks.
audience: People who value health without sacrificing fun
occasions: Morning or weekend brunch at cafés
Afternoon refreshment during coworking or meetings
Post-yoga, pilates or gentle movement session
As a daily treat or accessory for healthy routines

voice.tone: Bright, light-hearted, friendly, stylish but not pretentious, casually uplifting. Use clear, easy language with warmth and a hint of aspiration.

visual.mood: Minimal, pastel, serene. Calm energy that feels modern, with subtle pops of tropical color for approachability.
visual.photography: Bright, natural light. Candid, real-life moments at cafés, beaches, parks – people enjoying the drink with food or friends, never overly staged or posed.
visual.typography: Elegant serif for the wordmark (Didot/GFS Didot), clean sans-serif for body (Montserrat), expressive script (Brittany Signature) for detail and warmth.
rejectLooks: Dark, moody, or neon energy drink palettes

inLineBrands.name: POPP
inLineBrands.why: Focuses on wellness and refreshing taste with a bright, pastel, minimal aesthetic—mirrors PEP’s approach to being a treat for healthy habits.

inLineBrands.name: RITA
inLineBrands.why: Emphasizes natural, probiotic, and lifestyle-driven benefits. Uses hip, modern designs with earthy, pastel colors, making it approachable in wellness occasions.

inLineBrands.name: AUNE & KEVAI
inLineBrands.why: Serene, pastel design and a rejuvenating, health-focused proposition ideal for the lifestyle upgrade set.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Positioned as a hydration/energy functional drink for people chasing specific physical goals; more functional and less lifestyle-aspirational than PEP.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Focused on BCAA energy and fitness routines (gym market), lacking the lifestyle and everyday wellness positioning PEP aims for.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROSIP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Combines protein and alcohol with a functional, bold look targeting gym goers or party/fitness fusions, not lifestyle or wellness upgrade.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: FISHERS ISLAND
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Geared toward the social drinking set, replacing cocktails and positioning for nightlife or party occasions; too event-specific for lifestyle routine.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LYRE'S
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Non-alcoholic spirits for replacing social drinks — focuses on the ritual of social occasions rather than daily wellbeing. More about social mimicry, less about everyday health.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: RECIPES
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Cocktail and fun beverage positioning for people wanting excitement; not centered on wellness or lightness for daily healthy habits.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body/gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Protein-focused functional drinks with aggressive, masculine gym-bro branding and bulk imagery; the opposite of a stylish lifestyle drink.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision (US probiotic/energy drink); overlaps but does not fit the premium, lifestyle wellness positioning and could confuse market identity.

brandFitRules: Does the brand or design use minimal, pastel, or earthy tones? (YES for fit)
Does the copy reference wellness, balance, refreshment, or daily routines? (YES for fit)
Is the target context a relaxed, stylish or wellness-aspirational moment (e.g. brunch, café, coworking, gentle recovery)? (YES for fit)
Does the visual identity avoid bold, aggressive or neon gym/energy drink codes? (NO gym-bro or techy looks allowed)
Does the copy avoid words associated with heavy workouts, maximizing gains, or sports nutrition? (NO functional/protein-bro language)
Is the overall impression approachable, inviting, and visually calm—not nightlife or festival-centric? (YES for fit)
